sentences.text: The doctor is checking with the nurses at the desk, to see if Connie and John have arrived at the hospital.

The doctor is in his office with Connie, when her phone goes off, with an important text, which turns out to be bad news about a murderer that has escaped from the hospital. The murder killed a police officer after escaping.
The doctor and Connie discuss the news from the text and John is devasted by the news, because he knew the police officer.
The doctor is explaining to Connie the repercussions of the news she received in the text and how many people are now in danger. John is still trying to process the news.
Connie doesn't like what he's saying and gets angry. She goes after the doctor and shoves him over in a fit of rage.
Shea is in his office. He is a movie producer.
Christian sits down in Shea's office to pitch a script he and Bradley have written together.
Shea is listening intently while Christian makes his pitch. The pitch is for a gangster movie in the vein of The Godfather. Shea is a bit confused since this seems like a copy.
Christian goes on and goes more in depth into what he is talking about.Shea continues to listen.
Christian starts to get into the nitty gritty and describe exactly why Shea should make this movie.
Shea still doesn't get it. Christian is a bit frustrated and pulls off his glasses to have a more direct connection with Shea.
At this moment, Shea's secretary interrupts the meeting to bring Christian's partner into the meeting. Christian is relieved as Bradley is a much better pitch man. He is just generally more charming.
Christian quickly describes Bradley's background to Shea and then gets up
Christian gets up and invites Bradley in to meet Shea. Bradley it Christians secret weapon usually in his pitches and as a director this is how he gets his movies made.
Denzel, a middle-aged man, follows an elderly man through a large room filled with various items, such as shelves filled with books.
Denzel stands still next to a painting. The painting depicts an large, old ship on the sea, like those from the colonial era, with many sails.
The elderly man explains the value of the painting and the importance of everyone keeping it safe.
A young woman, Mila, silently listens to the elderly man's story.

Two other middle-aged men, Tom and Gary, are seated at a large table nearby, also listening to the old man's tale, worried about what they're hearing and whether or not they will be able to keep the painting safe.
James was on the phone call with the person who he owed all that money to. They once again told him that they wanted their money and they would get it one way or another.
James had went away and was hold up in an unassuming brick building that someone turned into an apartment. He thought he could hide out there but he was being surveilled.
Despite hiding out, James was receiving these phone calls with threats about harming hum and getting their money. They always scared him very much.
James was so scared that he always kept the place very dark. He did not want anyone to know someone was inside.

James would sit and reflect all the time about how he ended up in this situation. He got too far in debt with the wrong people and now they are here to claim what is theirs. He is trying to find a way out.
Its very tough on him right now as he was injured on the job and is left to move around in a wheelchair. So if anyone comes after him, he will not be able to get away at all.
As he rolls around in this tiny apartment, it becomes more difficult for him to cope with his situation. He has seemingly lost it all, including his ability to move around.
All James has left is himself and it makes him sit around and think a lot. He has nothing better to do at this point in time.
James always makes sure that the door is securely locked so no one can get in and do more damage to him.
Jennifer approaches her father, Gary, while he is in his study. He has been working for the last few hours and she's waited all this time anxiously. She wants to ask him a question.
Gary hears her approaching and looks up from his work. He is mildly amused that she would come to him while he is in his study, which is unusual for her.

She gathers her strength and after getting his attention, asks him if she can become his understudy. He works in law and she would like to work in the same field. She knows the only way to break into the career is to learn from her father.
There is tension in the room as the question hangs in the air. Neither say a word for a bit. The silence is deafening.
Gary takes in her request. He's very surprised she would ask such a question. He wants to appear like he's not surprised so he focuses on his work with indifference.

He then decides to make sure she is serious about this request. He asks her a witty question with a semi smile on his face "Are you sure you want to work for me? You barely listen to what I tell you to do here. What makes you think you'd do it at a workplace?"

A young woman, Natalie, and a young man, John, are standing in a large performance hall. The pair are ballet dancers and the hall is lined by mirrors with a grand piano in the corner. An older gentleman, Vincent, watches as they audition. He is the director of the ballet.
After a grueling dance session, Natalie looks to Vincent, anxiously awaiting any feedback.
Vincent looks down with a completely cold look on his face, completely ignoring Natalie.
Natalie's face looks horrified. She imagines that her performance was terrible and Vincent is deeply disappointed in her.
She hurries back to the dressing room dejected, where many other dancers, including her rival Ksenia, are waiting for their chance to audition.
The man stands in front of the mirror, hastily trying to get ready for work. He puts on his watch.
His wife asks if he can stay for a few minutes longer.
The man looks at his wife through the mirror and tells her that she knows he cannot miss any work. He is up for a big promotion.
The woman tries to tempt him by seducing him and he smiles in a coy manner. The man says he'll get back to that when he gets home.
He puts on his suit jacket and tells her things will be much better once he gets the promotion.
The parking garage she parks at every morning when she goes to work. only this time it's at night and she isn't going to workl
many hours later she meets her friend sally for breakfast. "Where's your car?" sally asks. Oh, you know, the usual place.
"Look, I'm not being completely honest. I didn't drive here, at least not today. I mean I've been in town all night. you know why"
sally: "look, it's none of my business, but if you really want to talk let's go into this diner where we can have some coffee and chat"

"the thing is, he talks in his sleep, and I can't help listening. This time he said intel already has a deal to buy nvidia, but the word is out so it's too late to get in. Then it got really interesting..." sally: "wow, you ain't kidding! Nvidia is up 50 per cent and intel is down 30 per cent! You're sleeping on a gold mine kid!"
Jack enters his hotel room after a long vacation day. He fears something doesn't feel right about the room he had just entered and is frozen in fear.
Jack goes to the bathroom to splash cold water on his face, only to notice that there is someone in the bathtub.
As Jack stands at the door of the bathroom, a woman emerges from the bathtub and stares at Jack.
The woman walks up to Jack and goes for an embrace. Jack hugs her back, as she tells him that she asked the front desk for the key to his room to surprise him.
Just as Jack was about to ask her a question, the woman appear to become much older and started to chase him around the room saying that she loved him and that they were meant to be together.
Jack backs away from the bathroom telling the woman that he is a married man and that she needed to leave.

Sam stood on a performance platform looking down at Jim, who had just purchased the casino. Jim was inspecting his recent purchase, making sure every inch of it was tidy. Sam hated what Jim had become. Jim was once a peaceful soul, but now he was starved for money.

Jim's wife told him not to do it. He thought for hours about how this purchase would dissapoint her. However, this was a way to finally live the dream life he'd always wanted. Casinos basically print money.
Jim then got a sharp pain in his stomach thinking about what would happen if his wife divorced him. He's be miserable without her.
As he continued to inspect the casino, he noticed a stranged device hidden in a small compartment in the casino walls. He flipped the switch on the device.
The switch caused many broght lights to come on. Jim was happy until he realized that the switch also released poison gas into the building, from which there was no escape.
George, a fantastically wealthy businessman, passes away after an illness.
A week later, Jacques, George's attorney, has gathered the two children and their respective attorneys together for an important meeting, which the family assumes will be the reading of the will.
Jacques reveals that for years George had a secret family in Jamaica. His frequent business trips were actually trips to see his other family.
Cary and Audrey, George's children, are stunned to speechlessness.
Jacques carefully explains that George wanted his entire estate to go to the other family. Cary and Audrey will get nothing.
Moses, a small yorkie, lies on its side on a hardwood floor in a home.
Ben stands beside him with a defensive posture, looking down at Moses with a look of worry and confusion.
Moses lunges and bites Ben's finger, causing him to scream in pain.
Ben, separated from Moses again, prepares to fight and motions for Moses to come at him with his hands.
Moses starts running towards Ben.
Moses lunges at Ben again, latching onto his crotch, as Cameron looks at the scene in horror.

In a Western town in the late 1800's, a sheriff stands glaring in the middle of the road through the center of town. He knows that a known convict is robbing the town bank and is determine to end this.
Inside the bank a man armed with a rifle is holding two men who work in the ban and a woman who was a customer hostage as he attempts to robs the bank.

Outside, the sheriff yells that he knows that the man is inside and what he is doing. He commands the bank robber to come out. The rifleman shouts back that he will shoot if anyone enters the bank as he raises the rifle and tries to fire a warning shot but nothing happened
He looks down to see his rifle was not loaded so he started load his rifle not aware that the sheriff can see him .
The sheriff raises his gun from behind the bank's bars and in a cloud of smoke shoots the robber taking him down.
Jim and Jeff are drinking at a bar.Jim, wearing a top hat and and orange tux, is getting drunk on wine. Jeff, dressed in a blue tux, is trying to decide what drink to have next.
Charles and his girlfriend Karen walk into the bar and look shocked. Jim is Karen's baby brother and was supposed to have tried to cut down on his drinking.
Jim, his hat off. turns to Jeff and smiles, Jeff amiles back sheepishly. Jeff looks guilty. Apparently, Jeff had talked Jim into having a frew drinks.
Trying to make Karen less angry at him, Jim glares at Jeff and blames him for causing him to fall off the wagon.
Jim leans back against the bar, looking sad. Jeff looks back as well. They are looking at Karen, leaving the bar in tears, accompanied by an angry Charles.
Hitmen mingle around with us all. You never know where you might bump into one. They speak in coded messages that they relay back and fourth, found in every country. Might be your neighbor.
Meet Jean and Tom. Just your average local hitmen. Always ready to go at a moments notice. Willing to stop what they are doing to end your problems, for a fee of course.
This time is different. This time they know who the target is. It doesn't make any sense though.
The payout though. That is a really tall number. Friends or not, this could actually be worth the heartache.
Jean is beside himself. Not so much that his friends are on the list, but that he is actually considering the job. That, is a lot of money.
Dane was very angry as he was driving into work because of phone calls he had just finished with Demi.
He made it to work and once he got to the gate he was having an issue with security. It seems that perhaps they were warned bout him and removed him from the elist of people to allow to drive in.
After his issue with security was solved, he was allowed to drive and he went to park on the top level where Demi was waiting for him and Ruben watched from afar.
He and Demi had a conversation in which they discussed their failed relationship that she wanted to end. She did a lot of the talking as he listened. Ruben was always not too far away.

Dane could not believe what he was hearing from the woman he loves. She was telling him that she no longer wants a future with him. He was not very happy to hear that. He has a lot of questions in his mind that he wants answered. Especially about Rubem and his role in this.

Jeff and Jim, 2 peas in a pod. The best of friends that do everything together. After one of them won the lottery, and of course sharing half of it with their friend, they are just living life to the fullest. Sitting there one night needing something to do, bored out of their mind.
Then Jim spots it. Something they have never have done, because they could never afford it.
Mingled with rich people at a massive party, limited to only those with money. They decided this was it, the fun they had been looking for.
You have to look the part though. Have to fit in with everyone else. They needed snob appeal.
After getting their hair all done, they were ready. Ready to mix it up with high society, and be one with their riches.
Armed and masked men patrol around an office in a high rise building, keeping an eye on the scared and startled workers.
Robert sits on the floor alongside some coworkers, hands behind their heads, cowering in fear.
The armed men take positions around the area, ensuring that they have control of all their hostages.
Meanwhile, Val rummages through a large vault with many safety deposit boxes, looking for his desired loot.
Val turns his attention back towards the main office area and approaches with his rifle raised up.
A young woman, Mary, is hanging up white sheets on clotheslines outdoors.
Nearby, her brother, John, is using an axe to chop some logs for firewood.
Their mother, Sue, comes outside of their old brick house and calls to Mary.
John watches them, and Sue tells Mary that she needs to fetch some water as fast as possible, because their younger sibling's fever is spiking.
Mary runs off to the nearby well as John stares off into the distance towards the well.
Mary fills up a pot with water from the well.
John looks down to the ground, deep in contemplation and worry over whether or not their brother will make it.
Mary takes the filled up pot and runs back towards the house as quickly as she can.
A young woman in an 18th century dress is sitting down, comforting her little sister in her lap. A boy leans against a tree.
Mel and his three sons (Heath is the oldest one) are approaching, walking in front of a horse and wagon, as well as a saddled horse.
Mel approaches the young woman, who is standing in front of a house that has burnt down.
Mel looks down in shock, apparently at his son, who has been killed. Heath and his brothers stand behind him.
Mel is looking at various toy soldiers.
Mel is saddened.
Heath looks on, grimly.
Mel's family are gathered around a newly dug grave.
During the Korean war, two medical teams formed football teams and played each other. The blue team kicked off against the M.A.S.H. team.
The coaches of the blue team watch confidently as their players go on the field.
The M.A.S.H. team looks weak and scrawny but still full of energy. They know they don't stand much of a chance.
The strong blue team successfully holds off the M.A.S.H. team from scoring a touchdown.
The M.A.S.H. team runs off the field after being stopped. The blue team looks menacingly at them to assert their dominance.

After a lifetime of research, he had finally done it. Phillip's life work was creating a device that could transport users into old photographs. He put on his best suit and transported himself into an old black and white photo. He was right there with his mother and her great-grandmother on her wedding day.

Phillip couldn't believe how real each experience was. He was able to enter any photo he could find. He was transported to the location and was right there. This time he jumped into a scene with three of his uncles. All looking good in suits.
His next trip was into a photo from the 1900s. He was able to meet and speak with his great-grandmother. A woman he had never actually met in real life.
Next he went back even further. He got a chance to meet and talk with his great-grandmother's extended family. This was like meeting your entire family tree in real time!

At the end of the day Phillip was exhausted, but felt exhilarated by this new technology. He took off his suit and began to catalog all of his old photos. He pinned them to the wall, making a map of all the new places he would explore. And smiled thinking about all of the new people within his family that he would finally meet for the first time.
Sara had been drinking all night. She couldn't remember much of what happened.
In fact, she wasn't sure how she made it from her bedroom to the living room this morning. She just sort of found herself sitting on the couch.

She concentrated on regaining some memory, at least of the past hour or so, however long she'd been awake. She slowly pieced it together: she had woken up, lit a cigarette, then rushed to the bathroom. Where was the cigarette now?
With panic, she remembered it falling from her fingers as she had dashed to the bathroom. And just then, she was sure she smelled smoke from a fire.
She ran to the bedroom which was filling with smoke. She could kiss her security deposit goodbye.
Brian stood in the dark office shocked by the events unfolding around him. He knew they were in trouble, but the fear that gripped him was very intense and made him catch his breath.
Suddenly Joan's phone rang. She answered it and it was the person they were hiding from on the other end. They were being hunted by this man and had no way to get away now.

She listened as the man described the room and told her who was in the room with her. His focus turned to Julia. He told her how Julia was his real target and all they had to do was make her leave and they would be free. Joan glanced at Julia but remained silent.
Julia saw Joan's gaze and looked at her questioning what was being said. She did not realize she was already in the crosshairs of the mans rifle.

Matt was perched on a roof on the next building and had Julia in his rifle sight as he spoke calmly with Joan and laid out his demands. Either they could make Julia leave, or they would all be killed.

Tom tried to log into the companies computer system to get word out to the police since the phone lines had been cut, but he quickly realized the system had been taken over as well and he had no access.
Joan tried to negotiate with Matt and find other ways to end the standoff and asked what else he wanted.
Julia realized that it was her he was after and that if she did not give herself up, everyone would be killed and she resigned herself to leave the room.
Joan realized that by Matt's description of the room he must be on the roof of the next building. She hung up the phone and turned to stare in his direction.
Driving a blue sedan, Amy pulled up in front of the house, which was located in an older neighborhood of neat frame houses.

Looking a bit dissheveled with his scruffy beard, crooked wire-rim glasses and paunch barely contained by the vest of his three-piece suit, Christian descended the home's porch stairs, clasping his chest as he approached Amy's car. A neat privet hedge provided contrast with Christian's appearance.
Seeing Christian's condition, Amy lurched from the driver's door, her beige trench coat unbuttoned to reveal a length of comely thigh and her flame-colored hair trailing loose in the air.
Christian buckled and would have collapsed to the ground, but Amy managed to catch him around the waist, struggling mightily to support his girth.
It took all of her effort to get Christian inside the car, but in her shock and concern, Amy seemed to have acquired nearly superhuman strength.
James and Kim are attending a dinner celebrating an opening of a new restaurant. He doesn't know her, but has gathered information about her from others.
He observes her sitting at a table. He feels a flicker of jealousy as she is sitting with another man. He is drawn to her and plans to meet her.
Kim and the other man appear to be having a disagreement about something at the table.
She gets up finally and leaves the restaurant, much to her companion's protests.
James quickly follows her out, making sure to keep a safe distance from her. He doesn't want her to notice him.

He hides behind a newspaper until she is a bit down the sidewalk. Then, he follows her. He slows down and offers her a lift. He does this in order to engage her in conversation and, hopefully, ask her out to dinner.

three executives of Funny Data Inc. are meeting to decide whether they should fire their top employee who is Eric Tomkins. the problem is that despite Eric's talents, he is simply making too much money and the company can't afford to employee him any more. their decision: they will give him the axe in 2 weeks.
Eric has received the bad news (in addition to a gold watch) and is now explaining the situation to his wife. Eric is so ashamed he can not even look at his wife.

Eric is furious. it is simply immoral to get fired when you are the top person at the company so he is talking to his union representative, Fran Kessinger. Fran is explaining that he is willing to approach the bosses but to save Eric's job, he will have to take a 30 percent pay cut.

Eric is stunned and is speechless. he can't fathom taking such a big pay cut; his wife doesn't make much money working at the Diner. besides, she loves going on shopping sprees. eventually Eric says he will accept a 10 percent pay cut.
Fran looks at the ground because he knows there is no chance that he can save Eric's job. there is zero chance the bosses will keep Eric on the payroll at only a 10 percent pay cut.
Charlotte is bored and spinning a top while she listens to the priest preach a sermon in church.
Some of the congregants are so bored by the sermon that they have actually fallen asleep.
The priest is oblivious to his bored congregants and continues to preach his sermon.
He drones on and on, as his congregants get more and more bored by the minute.
Charlotte sneaks a glance towards Thomas, who also looks like he is about to nod off as well. She doesn't blame him, as the sermon is really boring.
Jason and Dexter are together in a darkened room with a sofa chair. Nearby in another room, Dexter can be seen through a doorway, a drink in his hand.
There is a close up of Dexter standing near a portrait of a lovely lady with a solemn appearance.
Nick and Jason are looking at Dexter who is looking at Jason.
Jason is on a cell phone, appearing to be listening.
Jason now is in a car, still on the cell phone, apparently there because of the phone call. He appears to be in the front passenger seat.
The piano was dreadfully out of tune.
Jason had had enough with the piano. He had been paid a small fortune, for him, to perform tonight and was promised a functional piano.
Jason was alternatingly distraught and frustrated. He had to do something.
As they say, when the piano is not in tune, it is time to torture the tuner. At least that's what the voices in Jason's tormented head told him.
The tuner was exhausted. He couldn't tell an A sharp from a D minor.
Nick found this all very hilarious and could not stop laughing at Jason's misfortune.
Jason targeted Nick next, throwing a pint of water into his face.
Sam and Laurence would sometimes turn to their fatih in religion during intense moments during the war.
Occassionally the army would drop in livestock to remote areas as food for the soldiers.
After a late night BBQ the soldiers spent their time trying to relax before heading off into the jungle the next day.
A good meal really seemed to lift the moral of the younger soldiers at night.
Frederic was trained as a chef back home so he helped prepare a special gourmet meal for the men.
Albert and Laurence listened intensely to the stories of the veteran soldiers at night.
Martin tried to drown his sorrows and wartime memories with a few cold beers. That usually only helped for so long before it was back into the fray.
Everyone studied the map of the terrain before the morning's mission.
Opinions differed over the approach for the attack. The men were all nervous about what lay ahead for them in the night.
Ben froze in place. He was face to face with the thing he feared most. He took a step back, feeling panicked as his heart raced. There it was. His sister's Border Terrier. She called him Louie.
Ben slowly lowered his finger toward Louie. He tried to remain calm and pet Louie. But Louie was too excited. He leapt into Ben's arms. Ben let out a terrified scream.
His mother Lin and sister Cameron watched. The two had seen this before. But it never gets easier. All they could do was watch Ben struggle to pet the small dog.
Ben and Louie wrestled on the ground. Louie meant no harm, but Ben was too afraid to realize it. He screamed and cried. Usually it's the dog, but this time it was the man.

Lin yelled out for Louie. She couldn't bear to watch Ben suffer any longer. Louie calmly walked over to Lin. Ben just sat on the floor sobbing. It was always like this. Ben never got over his fear of dogs. It only got worse.
Two men enter old rickety house looking for joe. Joe is a competitor in their business. They hold their guns ready to shoot him when they see him.
Joe is caught off guard. He hides in a dark corner in the room hoping that the man do not find him.
The men discuss if they should enter the house to find Joe or if he's even here.
Meanwhile, Joe pulls out a gun, aiming it at the men, as he can see them from inside the house. He's a bit shaky as he knows they can spot him at any minute.
The men enter the house looking for Joe. They wonder if he's even there. Joe holds his gun steady.
As Matt is walking he sees Phillip with headphones on, listening to music in a booth.
As Matt continues walking past, he notices a man from further across the room. The man sees him approaching.
Matt stands there looking at him, contemplating what to do.
He walks back to the booth where Phillip is listening to music and waves.
Phillip slides back the door of the booth to talk to Matt.
Matt sees both Jude and Phillip inside the booth, huddled close together sharing one set of headphones to listen to the music.
Matt stands there, just outside the booth, slightly concerned and upset about what he is seeing.

Jude and Matt are walking together in a partially busy Grand Central Station during the daytime in New York City in the 1950s. Both are in their 20s and look young, good looking, and fresh. Jude is walking cool while wearing a stylish black hat, fitted dark blue jazzy suit, and white and black dress shoes. Matt seems a little nerdy, looking a little like a college professor, wearing black glasses, dark slacks, a light dress shirt, a tie and light brown corduroy dress jacket. Matt has white papers in each hand and is trying to look at one while walking, while Jude is looking straight ahead as he walks.
Matt stops to open a brown wooden box a little smaller than a suitcase and looks inside for something. He's trying to find the correct papers they need, but can't.
Jude seems agitated and uneasy, staring straight ahead with eyes wide open. The papers they need are train tickets for their upcoming journey, and Matt has somehow lost them.

Jude becomes nervous, his face shiny with sweat. What is he going to do now? They don't have money for more train tickets, but it was very important they got on that train. Matt looks at Jude, waiting for him to make a decision on how to proceed. Jude thinks things over.

Jude finally goes outside to the grey cobblestone street that's all wet and shiny after a rain. There are multiple scooters parked there waiting for their owners as well as a few people milling about. Jude is going to back to his apartment to think things over. He looks back at Matt, warning him with his eyes not to follow.
A large dinner is being held in a banquet hall in a castle. Two long tables are filled with people eating and conversing. A man stands near a buffet table filling his plate with food.
Eric leans towards the person sitting next to him at the table and begins gossiping about people at another table. He has a pained expression on his face as he talks about the person.
Eric's mind wanders as he watches the people he was talking about. He feels sadness for them because they are newcomers and don't know the ways of the court yet. He wants to reach out and help them.
The people Eric was gossiping about, Mark and David, sit together. They feel like they don't fit in with the rest of the court and don't realize they are being talked about.
A woman sees them sitting awkwardly and gives them a smile, wanting them to feel welcome. Another man has the same feeling and also offers Mark and David a smile as well.

Gerard gamely attempted conversation with his pretty passenger Jennifer, who awkwardly rifled through her purse as he pedaled the pedicab on the boardwalk past a pizza joint decorated with stripes in the color of the Italian flag.

Gerard pulled the pedicab and its passenger in front of a local landmark, the tacky, Trump branded Taj Mahal casino. Tourists passed along the walk, seemingly unimpressed by the casino's towering white pillars and kitschy marquee.
Jennifer, her skin bronzed by the sun, continued looking at her purse, while politely feigning interest in Gerard's rehearsed narration of the tour.
Gerard, wanting to make a connection on more than a surface level, confided in Jennifer that he only took this humble job in order to finance a grander ambition as a screenwriter.
The pedicab, with its striped awning and yellow cab, blended well with the tourists in their pastels and bright colors.
Kevin is an uber driver and is waiting for his fare to get in the car.
William gets in the back seat and introduces himself to Kevin.
Kevin asks william if he needed to make any other stops before his ultimate destination. he looks at william through his rear view mirror and william says, no, let's go.
kevin drives off and william is asking him if he could possibly drive any slower.
kevin floors the accelerator and speeds through an intersection. luckily there were no other cars on the road.the rest of the ride it seems as if kevin has an attitude.
when they reach william's destination, kevin gets out the car, stands in front of the vehicle, and yells at william through the front windshield "we're here".

After the attack between the north and south reaches a pause, a group of adults are waiting to receive medical attention. The older white male John is next in line to be seen and he watches on as a woman has a bandage wrapped around her head. A smiling man to John's right is happy that his head wound has been bandaged and the pain is receding.

Jude is out of surgery on his neck and has been laid in a bed to recover. He fades in and out of consciousness as the light shines down on his body, the sweat showing how hard his body fights to recover.

Nicole has finally made her way into the hospital, taking in the scene and adults that await medical attention. She looks over and sees Jude laying in the bed and it hits her just how close he came to death.
Nicole grips a hand for support as she leans over to check if Jude is awake or asleep. She worries that he might not wake up even though the doctor has told her the main risk has passed.

As Jude starts to cough, Nicole's mind assumes the worst and no matter what the doctor has communicated, her mind continues to fear that Jude is going to die. She stares at his face, fear in her mind and wills his eyes to open so that she might alleviate her worries at last.
Ellen looks down with a look of determination on her face - she has finally found the courage she needed to leave her abusive husband.
Robert has fallen to the floor and his hands are raised over his head - he was not ready for this attack.

Robert tries to rise from the floor, kitchen chairs and tables overturned but Ellen holds the bat that she has already hit him with once. Leonardo cowers behind her, knowing that he will get the ire of Robert's revenge.
Ellen threatens to hit him with the bat again while a beaten and bruised Leonardo stands behind her in anguish.
Robert starts yelling and waving his hands after he gets up. He is very upset and focusing his comments on Leonardo, telling him he is a loser and worthless.
Leonardo, having found courage as well, yells back at him, finally telling him how hurt and abused he as felt all these years and how he is truly the one who is worthless.
Robert has a red and evil face as he approaches Ellen and Leonardo, yelling and frothing at them both, telling them they are nothing without him.
Ellen pushes Leonardo away as Robert raises his fists - she is still holding the bat and ready to swing again to protect her son.
Sam stares at the fallen moose wondering what exactly could have caused this.
Brooke leads Sam away and tries to help figure out what was going with the moose when it fell.
When Sam reaches the beach he sees Linda being attacked by a giant eel.
He grabs Linda in order to save her from the eel.
After the eel is gone Linda dances around happily with her dog in the water enjoying the water.
Sam ends up playing with the dog in the water enjoying knowing they are all safe now.
a woman in a dark room appears frightened by something.
four young people are intently looking at something on a laptop. There are some long shadows as the room they're in is somewhat dark,
the four people are standing and visiting and something has amused Matthew because he's smiling broadly.
Matthew, Jonny, and Angelina are talking.
Fisher is on a computer in a room with strange lighting recessed in the walls.
Fisher is saying something to another in the room. The topic appears pleasant to him.
A young boy stares at the clouds, thinking about his crush. She's coming for a visit that day! They'll be leaving soon to pick her up.
A young girl mirrors the young boy's action, thinking about HER crush. She's excited that she's going to go visit him today! They'll be arriving soon to pick her up.
A terrible car accident has taken place. Emergency personnel surround the scene. A mangled car sits atop piles of broken glass.
At the hospital, a frantic mother learns that her son has perished in a car crash, along with his young female friend. Her ex-husband had been driving—and had also been drunk.
Her ex-husband tearfully apologizes and begs for forgiveness. He doesn't receive much sympathy.
The ex-husband confesses that he had been drinking because his lover had left him. He can't get his lover—and the two dogs he owned—out of his mind.
He thinks back to happier days and wishes he could escape back to them somehow.
He thinks about how happy he once was and how sad it is that he will most likely never feel that way again. All he can feel now is regret.
Harrison is carefully making his way down a muddy slope towards a river. He has to navigate the river bank and find a way through various trees and other vegetation.
Harrison finds a vine hanging from above and grabs it for an attempt to swing across the river instead of trying to wade or swim.
The vine didn't work to make it across the water and Harrison has to swim. He finds an object floating and grabs it.
Harrison lets go of the object and swims, against the current, towards his destination.
A group of natives carrying bows aims at Harrison as he continues to swim towards his plane. Some of the natives wade into the water while aiming their bows while the rest stay on the bank.

Harrison's plane is floating on the water. Harrison reaches the plane and lays underneath it resting from his swim. Natives hidden in the trees along the river bank watch Harrison while aiming at least one weapon at him.
Daniel is quite liquored up. He is sweaty and a bit angry. He is the head of a political machine like Tammany in 19th century New York.
The camera scrolls out and you see a full view of the scene.

Daniel is sitting next to Gary who is the money man behind Daniel's political machine. Gary has just stopped in for a few minutes as he doesn't like to be too deeply involved with the thuggery. That's why he still has his hat and coat. Daniel is dressing down John for failing his mission.
John is explaining himself to Daniel.
Daniel is very aggressively refuting John's excuses for not successfully pulling off the vote rigging.
Daniel is done laying down the law and gives one more menacing look to John before he delivers his final judgement. Gary still looks on with his face a blank mask.
Reese had an immediate answer when Matthew, the teacher, asked the class to name some of the most important consequences of the invention of the Guttenberg press.
Reese always has an answer to whatever question Matthew asks, and he is reluctant to call on her again, but no one else in the class has anything to contribute.
Reese answers that if it weren't for the Guttenberg press, high school yearbooks might never have come into existence.
She explains how they are really the most important books being printed because they capture the most meaningful events that shape students' lives.
She then mentions that she's the editor of this year's yearbook and she's able to take pre-orders for anyone interested.
On a bright and sunny day at the park full of people, Greta walks up to her husband Jake who is at the grill. She hands him a tray for the food.
Jake tells Greta that he invited his friend Ashton to the party and hopes that she doesn't mind.
Even though Greta doesn't like Ashton, she tells Jake she doesn't mind. But she does so with a smirk on her face. That's because Greta fails to tell Jake a secret.

The secret being is that Natalie, Greta's friend who Ashton has feelings for, is currently seeing another friend of theirs, a guy named Mark. And the two of them are by a table at the park helping to set it up.
Unfortunately, Ashton arrives at the park only to see the distressing sight.
Ashton proceeds to look down on his phone to text Jake that he couldn't make it.
The well dressed robber stood guard with his back against a wall and watched the people in the room intently. Though his face was covered with a mask, the impatience was still visible in his eyes.
He quickly turns when a man speaks to him from the floor. Office workers behind him look shocked and afraid.
The man who spoke is a hostage sitting on the floor in a group of people. He looks afraid and covers his face as the robber speaks harshly to him.
Robert, who is also a hostage, watches intently while sitting on the floor with his hands behind his head in another group of people.
Val has opened the safe and is preparing to steal assets from the company. He is also well dressed and masked and has a black leather bad to haul off the goods that they steal.
Val reaches the back of the vault and finds the door he has been searching for. He bends slightly and reaches for the doors handle anxiously awaiting what is behind it.
A man with a gun holds up his wounded friend as he moves through the street, brandishing his weapon.
Several armed policemen are chasing the man, and yelling "Get down!" as citizens run from the fray.
The armed man fires his automatic weapon at the police officers across a parking lot as they duck for cover.
The armed man crouches down and runs away, still holding his injured friend over his shoulder.
Two officers bend down and take the pulse of a third officer, who has been shot and is lying on the ground while stunned and terrified civilians look on.

A high level meeting with a crime family is happening. Colin is the new guy but slowly making his way into the organization. All the higher ups are standing around the main man, the boss. Jaime walks into the room.
Jaime approaches Colin, who is seated at a table. With a confident swagger, Jaime comes to the table.
Jaime sits at the table next to Colin. They face two men, one is the boss who is seated across the table and one is a henchman.
Jaime and Colin listen intently to the boss man as he speaks directly to them, slowly and deliberately. Around the room, goons stand behind Jaime and Colin in the back of the room.
Jaime, always the clown, cracks a wise quip in response to the boss and stares at him with a slight glare.
Daniel is informing Christoph that they have captured the prisoner, Brad, and that they have confiscated the item he is holding, from Brad.
Christoph goes to confront Brad. He tells him that his plans have been stopped and that Brad needs to tell Christoph who sent him.
Brad says that he will never tell Christoph who sent him and that there is no way he will give up the information.
Christoph begins threatening Brad and tells him he will be tortured and killed, if he doesn't give up the information.
Christoph hits Brad in the face and threatens him again. He says this is only the beginning and by the time their done torturing him, he will reveal the information.

A man in a green overcoat stands outside a fenced in church. A white cross stands just out side the front door of the church. The man is holding a microphone in front of his mouth and is reporting on who may be holed up in the church.
Charles Manson with his crazy look is suspected of being in the church after escaping prison earlier in the day and gunning down four officers not a mile away from the spot.

Fbi agents are down the road from the church looking over evidence of more crimes committed by Manson. Two look under a sheet as police stands by behind them. Here is the spot four officers were killed.
The reporter has already photographed the bodies of four law men gun downed by Manson in his escape from prison. The four officers were caught unaware in the spot the FBI is going over evidence.
The mayor after being informed of Manson's threat to his life gets he and his assistant a cup of coffee with a police escort across the street.
The woman is seen prior to the engagement.
Another woman has a casual meeting with a man. They are dressed elegantly, for a night out.
The two women talk with each other when they get together, during the night out with the group.
The original women then enters to rejoin the party indoors with the other guests. The other guests are inside the main house, while the two women were chatting in the back patio.
The woman sits around a table with the other guest, seemingly questioning someone on the other end of the table.
It's 9 PM, the night's just getting started. A woman gazes towards her friend. Tonight's not any ordinary night, it's a celebration.
The friend returns with two glasses of champagne. Cheers!
The woman moves inside and strikes up a conversation. It quickly turns into a heated debate.
The friend is fully enjoying the show and tries to take a drag from her neighbors cigarette
The man pulls away his hand. Not tonight. It makes the friend want that cigarette even more. She cannot believe the man has no interest in her, but in her friend.
Wolf stares out past the crowd of people on the ships deck to the open waters in the distance
the ships crew gather and shout, its been a long two months at sea and they are ready to disembark.
Full of himself, Paul enters carrying an enormous smirk. He has absolutely no fear about anything.
staring down the barrel of a submachine gun, Paul takes a stand against the guards.
He came for one thing and one thing only. He's not leaving without the hostage, he's not leaving without his wife.
A young man (SHIA) sits at a table across from another man. The young man is distressed—he's not used to being interrogated, but that's what's happening right now.
The other man (BILLY), older than the first, reaches into his pocket and produces a piece of paper.
Billy tells Shia that he has a transcript of a confession that Shia made when he was unaware he was being recorded.
Shia is doubtful and tells Billy that he doesn't believe him. He's certain that the man across from him is bluffing.
Billy begins reading the page out loud, and recounts Shia's unwitting confession word for word. It's clear that Shia has been exposed.

John and Mary are sitting in a cheap local diner in the early 2000s. John is in his early 30s and is shaggily handsome with scruffy, soft medium straw blonde hair and is wearing a black leather jacket. They're sitting opposite each other by the diner's long windows, which have a yellow sign on them and outside of which there's a view of a rundown tilted parking lot, trees and a white cement retail type building. John is leaning onto the table in between them looking intently at Mary, waiting for her to decide.

She is beautiful. Mary's in her late 20s, thin and has lighter blonde hair in a messy bob, and is wearing a fitted white tank top with a butterfly outline on front. Her dark bra is visible through the tank top. An old two-story grey cement motel is behind her outside the diner windows. She has a black mug of coffee in front of her and is slouching a bit while looking sharply into John's eyes, considering things.

John's patience is wearing thin as he waits. Suddenly he leans back heavily onto his chair and his face looks confused and undecided as he reaches his hand into the inside of his jacket to pull out money to pay.

At the same time, Rocco comes into the diner and stops on the other side at the entrance beside the old green and black cash register. He's about 35, Italian, with thick dark black eyebrows and slicked back black hair. He's tall and thin, wearing a black dress shirt with a dark dressy jacket over it. He has a serious, dour look on his face, and turns his head to stare at John and Mary. He's been following them.
Mary doesn't notice him and instead is still focusing on John. Clouds have covered the sun outside and now everything seems darker and greyer. She has decided.
Jeff halts his horse and prepares to dismount. He is having problems getting off because he is drunk.
Jeff dismounts clumsily -- almost falling to the ground -- as Hailee and Matt both on horseback look on.
Jeff staggers as he reaches the ground. Hailee and Matt are amused by the old drunk.
Matt asks Jeff if he needs some help.
Jeff, embarrassed, pulls out his gun and tries to to shoot Matt but the gun isn't loaded.
Calmly Matt takes out his gun, inspects it, and contemplates about what he will do to the old man.

Emile lays with his head on the table, exhausted after researching all night for an important piece of information. The sun is shining through a window down on his sleeping head. He knows that he is close to making an important discovery that will tie his research together. Before falling asleep he was close to putting the pieces together but needed one more clue before falling asleep.

As Emile wakes up, a sentence that he'd read last night flashes into his mind and suddenly all of the pieces fall into place. He reaches up towards the table for his book. The trees and grass can be seen through the door in the daytime.

Emile intently reads the book titled Tanaina Plantlore. His body has not been feeling right after his most recent foraging session in nature. His home is in disarray, mirroring the state within his own head. His hair is disheveled as he searches for the piece of information that is needed.

As Emile reads, he finds the piece of information that he needs, identifying a plant that he'd recently consumed. The pain on his face matches the pain felt in his body. He has worked so hard to survive off of the land, his hands still dirty from the recent foraging trip and his body has been pushed to his breaking point.

As Emile realizes what the plant was that he recently consumed, the situation turns dire. The plants that his hunger forced him to eat were actually poisonous. The pain in his stomach, the weakness that he feels in his body, the mental fogginess are all signs of his impeding death. He raises his hand to cover his mouth to hold in the scream he wants to emit. The horror in his eyes point to this knowledge that his time on Earth is limited. He gave it his all to survive in the wilderness but one mistake is all that it took to undo all the work he put in to survive.
Tom hears the voice of a familiar person behind him come into the room.
Tom turns around and stares in partial anger and partial disbelief.
Bill, Tom's former commander, looks down in what appears to be slight shame as Tom stares at him.

Tom looks in disgust as this commander betrayed him and his entire squad years ago and got away with it. Terrence and Christian stand in the background. They are Tom's second and third in command respectively, of his new squad.
Bill stands up and starts to talk, but Tom cuts him off angrily.
Bill turns around to walk out of the room since Tom will not listen. They will never reconcile as Bill had hoped.
